title: TLS Verification Disabled
description: Disabling TLS/SSL certificate verification exposes data in transit to MITM attacks, violating SOC2 CC6.7 (transmission of information). This is a critical misconfiguration often introduced during development and left in production.

finding.message: TLS verification disabled via '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}. This allows MITM attacks.

references: Never disable certificate verification in production code.
For development against self-signed certificates, install the certificate into your trust store instead of disabling verification.
Use environment-specific configuration that enables verification for non-development environments.
NIST SP 800-52 Rev 2 provides TLS configuration guidance.
